title: WHERE Clause
content:
### WHERE Clause

The WHERE clause filters rows based on specified conditions. Only rows that satisfy the condition are included in the result.

---

### Basic WHERE Syntax

```sql
SELECT name, salary
FROM employees
WHERE salary > 60000;
```

### Comparison Operators

| Operator | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| = | Equal to | WHERE name = 'John' |
| <> or != | Not equal to | WHERE status <> 'inactive' |
| > | Greater than | WHERE salary > 50000 |
| < | Less than | WHERE age < 30 |
| >= | Greater than or equal | WHERE price >= 100 |
| <= | Less than or equal | WHERE quantity <= 10 |

### Text vs Numeric Values

Text values must be enclosed in single quotes. Numeric values are written without quotes.

```sql
-- String comparison
SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Electronics';

-- Numeric comparison
SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 500;

-- Date comparison
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date >= '2024-01-01';
```

> **Key Point:** String comparisons in WHERE are case-insensitive in some databases (MySQL) but case-sensitive in others (PostgreSQL with default settings).

quiz.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter rows based on a condition?
How should text values be enclosed in a WHERE clause?
